purpose: Write and extend tests using Node's built-in node:test runner with zero test-framework dependencies, following a fixture-over-mock style for external processes.
useWhen: Adding or extending test coverage in a Node.js project that uses node:test instead of jest/vitest
dontUseWhen: The project uses jest, vitest, mocha or another test framework instead of node:test
The change is not testable via node:test (e.g. it needs a browser environment)
requiredInputs: the module or behavior under test
the project's existing test file conventions (see project/codex-plugin-test-conventions)
procedure: Prefer a fixture (a fake implementation of the external dependency) over a mocking library when a test needs to stand in for a process, server, or filesystem
Keep each `test()` block focused on one behavior; name it as a full sentence describing that behavior
Run `node --test tests/*.test.mjs` locally and confirm the new test passes before submitting
verificationSteps: `npm test` (or the project's equivalent node --test invocation) passes with the new/changed test file included
The new test fails if the change under test is reverted (sanity-checked before submission)
doneWhen: The new/changed test file passes under node --test
No test framework dependency was added to package.json
escalateWhen: The behavior under test cannot be exercised without a dependency this skill forbids (e.g. requires jest-specific mocking)
An existing fixture does not cover the external dependency being tested and building one is out of the task's declared scope
